name: agent-ready-installs
positioning: Install production SaaS infrastructure as editable source.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-api-saas
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs API keys, usage, credits, billing, docs, and webhooks as editable source.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-cloudflare-saas
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs a Cloudflare-native runtime, storage, queue, workflow, security, and deploy path.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-minimal-saas
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs a small SaaS foundation before heavier provider decisions.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-enterprise-saas
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs enterprise auth, access, compliance, support, and incident surfaces.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-ai-saas
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs an AI product surface with metering, quotas, and cost controls.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-customer-intelligence
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs customer health, event ingestion, usage, and risk visibility.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-integration-marketplace
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs connected accounts, provider adapters, integration health, webhooks, and sync visibility.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-security-center
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs a workspace security posture, data lifecycle, secrets, dependency, and audit control center.

oneShotPrompts.name: one-shot-support-ops
oneShotPrompts.useWhen: The app needs support, feedback, incident, on-call, postmortem, status, and health workflows.

safeWorkflow: Run stackfoundry add recipe <name> --dry-run before writing files.
Review module manifests, source files, dependencies, env notes, and maintenance skills.
Install only after the diff is accepted.
Run the target app's Biome lint/format, TypeScript typecheck, migration, test, production build, and responsive smoke-check workflow.
Never commit secrets or local provider metadata.
nextAppRouterRules: Use route groups such as (marketing), (auth), (app), and (admin) to share layouts without changing URLs.
Keep public Route Handlers under app/api and do not colocate route.ts with page.tsx in the same segment.
Keep reusable business logic in src/lib/stackfoundry and schema in packages/db/src/schema instead of duplicating logic inside route files.
Use catch-all or optional catch-all routes only when one renderer or dispatcher truly owns nested paths with the same auth/runtime boundary.
Keep high-risk handlers such as billing webhooks, OAuth callbacks, deletion/export APIs, and provider-specific signature verification explicit.
reviewChecklist: Are target paths correct for this app?
Are env vars documented without committing values?
Are schema and migration changes reviewed?
Are tests/checklists included for installed modules?
Did the target app pass its normal checks?
